title: Care HMIS / TeleICU
summary: Technical Architect for CARE HMIS & TeleICU deployments on the Open Health Care Network (CARE) platform.
description: Serving as Technical Architect for CARE HMIS & TeleICU deployments at eGovernments Foundation, contributing to the design and evolution of healthcare solutions on the Open Health Care Network (CARE) platform.

Design scalable features, reusable plugins, backend services, APIs, and integration patterns for hospital and public health workflows. Built integrations with eAushadhi, DVDMS, and Tele-Radiology solutions supporting multiple PACS/DICOM servers to enable interoperable healthcare systems.

Collaborate with product, engineering, clinical, and implementation teams on solution design, architecture reviews, technical decisions, and POCs. Create architecture documentation, API specifications, and release guides while developing reusable components, shared utilities, and engineering standards to improve platform scalability, maintainability, and developer productivity.

Key integrations include DICOM/PACS for radiology (using DCM4CHEE, OHIF Viewer, fo-dicom), pharmacy systems (eAushadhi API, DVDMS API with trigram-based fuzzy matching), and multi-architecture Docker builds for deployment flexibility.

title: Digit Studio
summary: Configurable low-code/no-code Service Delivery Framework built to accelerate development of government digital services on the DIGIT platform.
description: Served as the Technical Architect for DIGIT Studio, a configurable low-code/no-code Service Delivery Framework built to accelerate the development of government digital services on the DIGIT platform.

Designed the end-to-end platform architecture across frontend and backend, including configurable UI, backend services, APIs, runtime engine, extension framework, and integration patterns. Established reusable architecture, engineering standards, and configuration models that enable rapid development of citizen services with minimal code.

Led technical design, architecture reviews, requirement analysis, and implementation guidance while mentoring engineering teams and driving key technical decisions. Created architecture documentation, API specifications, developer guides, and release documentation.

Successfully delivered one of the first implementations for the Government of Djibouti, building a Building Plan Approval solution that validated the framework's scalability, configurability, and rapid delivery capabilities.

title: HCM Console
summary: Health campaign management platform simplifying large-scale field operations across African regions, reducing months of coordination to hours.
description: The HCM Console was built to simplify and accelerate health campaign management, helping organizations plan, execute, and monitor large-scale field operations — especially across African regions, where campaign logistics are complex and time-sensitive.

What traditionally took months to coordinate can now be done within hours using the console. Key capabilities include: configuring campaign screens for field workers, defining checklists and workflows for compliance, setting targets at boundary and user levels, configuring delivery rules and product assignments, generating user credentials, and monitoring real-time data on stock, deliveries, and progress.
Provides interactive dashboards and reports for supervisors and stakeholders, significantly enhancing operational efficiency, accountability, and real-time visibility.
This solution transformed how health campaigns are executed at scale, enabling better coordination across distributed field teams.

title: Digit Workbench
summary: Centralized master data management module using JSON Schema Draft 7 for dynamic UI generation across DIGIT applications.
description: Worked on the Digit platform to enhance configurability and streamline management of master/configuration data used across applications and services.

Designed and developed a centralized module that allows other applications and services built on Digit Core to define and manage their masters using JSON Schema Draft 7, enabling dynamic UI generation based on the schema.

The module improved productivity, reduced duplication, and ensured consistency across projects. Widely adopted in multiple projects, including Works, Mgramseva, Urban, Health Campaign Management, PUCAR, SELCO, and more.
Enables configuration-driven development, reducing time to market for new services and improving maintainability.

title: Digit Works (MUKTA)
summary: Employment scheme management platform for urban poor in Odisha, enabling transparent wage payments and project execution.
description: Contributed to the design and development of MUKTA (Mukhyamantri Karma Tatpara Abhiyan), a government initiative providing employment to the urban poor and improving Odisha's employment rate through community development.
MUKTASoft is a customized version of the base Works platform, tailored specifically for the MUKTA scheme with MUKTA-specific configurations and UI screens.
Designed reusable services and UI components/organisms, enabling screens to be dynamically generated from configurations. This increased productivity and reduced bugs and testing effort.

Key modules developed include: Estimation, Contract management, Attendance and billing. Enabled urban workers to receive wages directly in their bank accounts with SMS notifications, improving payment speed and transparency.
Implemented smart payments for contractor bills and vouchers. Successfully deployed in Odisha, enabling seamless execution of MUKTA schemes across the state.
MUKTASoft continues to streamline scheme execution, making government employment projects more efficient, transparent, and impactful.

title: Digit Urban
summary: Urban governance platform serving 450+ urban local bodies across India for citizen services like Property Tax, Trade License, and more.
description: As part of the Product Engineering team at eGovernments Foundation, contributed to the development of multiple DIGIT Urban modules, including Property Tax, Trade License, Fire NOC, Building Plan Approval, mCollect, Complaints, and Birth & Death Registration.

Built applications using a custom React framework, developing reusable UI components, shared libraries, and configurable frameworks that accelerated feature delivery and improved maintainability across products.
Designed and developed configurable, reusable dashboards for Urban modules, enabling analytics and monitoring across multiple services.

Led frontend development, managed and mentored a team of three developers, conducted code reviews, and drove engineering best practices. Contributed to platform security improvements, proof-of-concepts (POCs), release management, deployment support, technical documentation, and onboarding materials.

Supported multiple Urban implementations, including UPYOG and NUDM deployments, by mentoring implementation teams, resolving technical challenges, and promoting reusable, scalable engineering practices across the DIGIT platform.
